input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on a clean macOS machine:

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compilers, Git, core CLI utils |
| **JDK 17** | Bundled via MAUI workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android build toolchain |

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

Download from **[dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)** — get the latest .NET 8 (LTS) or .NET 9 SDK (current). The installer handles `PATH` setup.

Verify:
```bash
dotnet --version
```

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in Android SDK, iOS build support, and the MAUI templates. To discover available versions:

```bash
dotnet workload search maui
dotnet workload list          # shows what's installed
```

---

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload auto-installs the Android SDK (typically to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`). To manage it explicitly:

- Accept licenses: `dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net8.0-android -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true`
- Or install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for the SDK Manager GUI.

---

## 5. iOS Setup

After installing Xcode:
```bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
```

Open Xcode → Settings → Platforms → download an iOS Simulator runtime.

---

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the .NET MAUI extension is now the recommended free option on macOS.

---

## 7. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
dotnet workload list                 # confirm maui is installed
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui         # scaffold a project
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Android (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

Quick sanity check:
```bash
dotnet new maui --list   # should show MAUI templates
xcrun simctl list        # should list iOS simulators
```

---

## 8. Discovering Correct Versions

| What | How |
|------|-----|
| Supported .NET version | [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) |
| Required Xcode version | `dotnet workload` output & [MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) |
| Android API levels | Check `<TargetFrameworks>` in the `.csproj` template |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → verify with a new project. The workload handles JDK + Android SDK automatically.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they need a reference guide. Let me provide a comprehensive answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI development setup on macOS.
